facts: Alice is the sibling of Bob.
Bob is the sibling of Carol.
query: Is Alice the sibling of Carol?
why: sibling_of is symmetric, not transitive — a sib b, b sib c does NOT give a sib c; must refuse

facts: Alice is the parent of Bob.
Bob is the parent of Carol.
query: Is Alice the parent of Carol?
why: parent ∘ parent = grandparent ≠ parent — parent_of is not transitive; must refuse

facts: The box is left of the cup.
The cup is left of the lamp.
query: Is the box left of the lamp?
why: left_of is not admitted as transitive (needs an explicit shared-frame / total-order proof); must refuse
mixed predicates do not compose — the less_than search walks only less_than edges; carol unreachable; must refuse
strict orders are irreflexive — a reflexive query (start == target) refuses; the conflicting cycle must not fabricate a result
disjoint chains — no contiguous path from alice to dan (missing middle); must refuse
inverse+transitive composition is out of scope for B2 — greater_than has no edges to chain and one-hop inverse is a single hop; must refuse
